content: XIIID
- The ratio of gold to the U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) broke-out in September 2024 from a 45-year downtrend-line.
- The outperformance of the gold miners versus the major U.S. stock market indices has broken-out, as suggested by rising bond-market inflation expectations.
■ Sensitive inflation indicators in the markets are starting to come alive.
- The U.S. Dollar appears to be entering a long-term downtrend, which is bullish for commodities and inflation.
■ Silver has outperformed gold. And the relative-performance of the silver miners has broken-out against almost every broad stock-market index in the world.
- Agricultural commodities and related-stocks are starting to rise, as several agriculture ETFs and fertilizer stocks have broken-out.
1. Gold is set to outperform the S&P 500 and international stock-markets for many years to come.
2. The shift toward hard assets is likely to gain further momentum.
3. China's stock-market could be among the best-performing equity markets in the world.

Kweichow Moutai, China's premium liquor maker, raised the ex-factory/ wholesale price of its flagship 53-degree Feitian Moutai to CNY 1,269 from CNY 1,169 per 500-ml bottle and increased the retail price from CNY 1,499 to CNY 1,539 per 500-ml bottle, effective March 31st. This is Moutai's first price hike in 30 months.

L'Oréal's China market sales growth accelerated from \(1\%\) year-over-year in 1H 2025 to \(5\%\) year-over-year in 2H 2025. Sales of North Asia—home to the China market—stabilized in 2025, increasing \(0.5\%\) year-over-year, reversing a two-year decline. According to CEO Nicolas Hieronimus, premium consumption in the China market is recovering, which has strengthened L'Oréal's confidence.

- The mainland market of Estée Lauder, a premium skincare brand, delivered organic growth of 13% year-over-year, registering the second consecutive quarter (ended December 31, 2025) of double-digit growth and making the Chinese market a key contributor to the group's overall recovery.
Nationwide, various home-appliance makers are reportedly planning a round of price hikes of about 10%, the first price hike since 2021.
- There also have been sporadic reports on price hikes for cement, a key input for real estate and infrastructure projects.

- Eaton (ETN, $385.58, see WILTW reports) manufactures several categories of grid equipment that the EMP Commission identified as critical to national recovery after a large-scale EMP event. These include transformers, switchgear, reclosers, surge arresters, protective relays, and substation-automation systems. Eaton offers a single position that captures EMP preparedness, climate adaptation, and electrification growth simultaneously.

- GE Vernova (GEV, $936.07, see WILTW reports) offers more concentrated exposure to the power-infrastructure buildout. Its Electrification segment, which includes the grid-solutions business, delivered $9.6 billion in revenue last year. This reflects GEV's growing-order backlog for switchgear, transformers, and AC substation equipment.

- Generac (GNRC, $202.93) manufactures backup power and distributed-energy resilience, which form the second tier of products needed in the event of an EMP attack and an extended grid outage. The EMP Commission

- Project for Public Spaces (PPS): Working with different local organizations, PPS transforms “dead” urban spaces into community gathering spots. In San Antonio, Texas, for example, it helped turn a concrete underpass into a dedicated teen hangout, complete with a shaded basketball court, ping pong tables, large picnic tables, and seating areas that encourage “loitering.”

- Log Off Movement: Launched in 2020 by a high school senior in response to her own struggles with social media, Log Off provides a toolkit for teens to build analog networks in their own towns. Its popular “Forks Up, Phones Down” campaign encourages teens to dine together to build authentic human connection.

CoGenerate: This national program matches teens with seniors to work together on projects that address different issues affecting their respective communities, from climate action to healthcare access.
